annotatedManual.description: No CORS restrictions. Server accepts requests from any origin, enabling cross-origin attacks.
annotatedManual.mitigation: Restrict CORS to specific trusted origins

annotatedManual.description: JWT tokens are not properly validated. Tampered tokens are accepted by the service.
Session IDs are not regenerated after authentication, allowing session fixation.
annotatedManual.mitigation: Regenerate session IDs on every login

annotatedManual.description: Login endpoint is vulnerable to SQL injection; attackers can bypass authentication.
User input is reflected without encoding, enabling stored/reflected XSS.
annotatedManual.mitigation: Sanitize and HTML-encode all user-controlled output

annotatedManual.description: No audit logs exist for authentication attempts, making forensic analysis impossible.
annotatedManual.mitigation: Implement comprehensive audit logging with tamper-evident storage

annotatedManual.description: Different error messages for valid vs invalid usernames allow user enumeration.
annotatedManual.mitigation: Return generic error messages for all authentication failures

annotatedManual.description: Critical security headers (X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, HSTS) are missing.
annotatedManual.mitigation: Add all recommended HTTP security headers

annotatedManual.description: File paths are not validated, allowing traversal to sensitive system files.
annotatedManual.mitigation: Validate and canonicalize all file paths before use

annotatedManual.description: No rate limiting or account lockout on login endpoint, enabling brute-force attacks.
annotatedManual.mitigation: Implement rate limiting and progressive account lockout

annotatedManual.description: No password complexity requirements; trivial passwords like '123456' are accepted.
annotatedManual.mitigation: Enforce strong password policy with complexity and length requirements
